subject: Gaming
title: Hades: Roguelike Perfection
description: A 60-second lesson on Hades: Roguelike Perfection.

hook.text: Hades turned death into a story mechanic. Every failure advanced the narrative. Dying was progress.

buildup.text: Supergiant Games had made three acclaimed games but wanted to try a roguelike—a genre about repeated death.

discovery.text: Each death sent you back to the underworld. New dialogue and story beats unlocked with every failed run.

twist.text: Hades launched in early access—unusual for a story game. Player feedback shaped the final product.

climax.text: Hades won over 50 Game of the Year awards. It proved roguelikes could have deep, emotional narratives.

punchline.text: Every death tells a story. Hades made failure feel like progress.

quiz.question: What made Hades unique among roguelike games?
options: Every death advanced the narrative, making failure feel like progress
